meta.question: What is the amount and typology of exception EX-2026-0142 for the Northwind account?

response.answer: [1] EX-2026-0142 is an open AML exception under structuring (sub-$10k cash deposits) typology, with a total amount of $47,900. [3] highlights that the pattern observed was consistent with structuring to evade reporting, which was escalated for SAR consideration as EX-2026-0142.

contexts.text:
# Northwind Trading LLC — Open AML Exceptions (2026 Q2)

Account: **NW-4471-08** · Risk rating: **High** · Reviewer: Compliance Desk

This summary lists the **open** AML exceptions for the Northwind account for the
current quarter. Three of the four open exceptions individually or in aggregate
exceed the **$5,000 SAR consideration threshold**; EX-2026-0142 also shows a
**structuring** pattern below the $10,000 CTR threshold.

| Exception ID | Opened | Typology | Amount (USD) | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| EX-2026-0142 | 2026-04-09 | Structuring (sub-$10k cash deposits) | 47,900 | Open |
| EX-2026-0155 | 2026-04-22 | Rapid movement of funds / pass-through | 312,000 | Open |
| EX-2026-0171 | 2026-05-11 | Counterparty in high-risk jurisdiction | 88,500 | Open |
| EX-2026-0188 | 2026-05-30 | Round-dollar wires inconsistent with profile | 9,800 | Open |

## Assessment
EX-2026-0142 (structuring) and EX-2026-0155 (pass-through) **breach the reporting
threshold** and warrant a Suspicious Activity Report. Recommend preparing a draft
SAR covering EX-2026-0142 and EX-2026-0155 for BSA Officer review. EX-2026-0171
remains under review pending counterparty screening (see restricted OFAC note).

# Northwind Trading LLC — AML Exception Register (Detail)

Detailed dispositions backing the Q2 exception summary. Compliance access only.

## EX-2026-0142 — Structuring
Between 2026-04-01 and 2026-04-08, eight cash deposits of $5,900–$6,100 were made
across three branches, aggregating **$47,900**. Each deposit fell below the
$10,000 CTR threshold. Pattern is consistent with **structuring** to evade
reporting. Recommended for SAR.

## EX-2026-0155 — Rapid movement of funds
An incoming wire of **$312,000** from a UAE counterparty was followed within 48
hours by outgoing wires to two Southeast Asian beneficiaries, leaving a near-zero
balance (pass-through). No documented commercial rationale. Recommended for SAR.

## EX-2026-0171 — High-risk jurisdiction counterparty
A **$88,500** wire involved a counterparty flagged for high-risk-jurisdiction
exposure. Disposition pending sanctions screening; see the restricted OFAC
screening note.

## EX-2026-0188 — Round-dollar wires
Four round-dollar wires totaling **$9,800**, inconsistent with the stated import
profile. Monitoring; not currently recommended for SAR.

# Transaction Review Note — Northwind Trading LLC (EX-2026-0142)

Reviewer: Compliance Desk · Alert: structuring · Account: NW-4471-08

## Observed pattern
The monitoring system flagged a burst of sub-threshold cash deposits. Branch and
timing detail:

| Date | Branch | Amount (USD) |
|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-01 | BR-12 | 6,100 |
| 2026-04-02 | BR-12 | 5,900 |
| 2026-04-03 | BR-07 | 6,000 |
| 2026-04-04 | BR-19 | 5,950 |
| 2026-04-05 | BR-07 | 6,050 |
| 2026-04-07 | BR-12 | 5,900 |
| 2026-04-08 | BR-19 | 6,000 |
| 2026-04-08 | BR-07 | 6,000 |

Total: **$47,900** across 8 deposits, all individually below the $10,000 CTR
threshold, spread across three branches within eight days.

## Conclusion
The pattern is a textbook **structuring** typology and has no documented
commercial justification consistent with the customer's import/export profile.
Escalated for SAR consideration as EX-2026-0142.

# AML Monitoring — Quarterly Desk Metrics (2026 Q2)

Aggregate, de-identified monitoring statistics for the commercial portfolio. No
single-customer case detail appears in this note (analyst clearance).

| Metric | 2026 Q1 | 2026 Q2 |
|---|---|---|
| Alerts generated | 1,284 | 1,412 |
| Alerts escalated to compliance | 96 | 121 |
| Accounts under enhanced monitoring | 38 | 41 |
| Median alert disposition time (days) | 6.2 | 5.4 |

## Commentary
Escalations rose 26% quarter-over-quarter, driven primarily by cash-structuring
typologies in the cash-intensive trade segment. High-risk accounts continue to
account for a disproportionate share of escalations. Specific account
identifiers and case narratives are restricted to compliance reviewers.

# Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) — Narrative Template

Use this template to draft the SAR narrative. The narrative must answer **who,
what, when, where, why, and how**. Do not disclose the existence of this SAR to
the subject.

## Header
- Filing institution: Atlas Bank
- Subject account: `{{account_number}}`
- Subject name: `{{subject_legal_name}}`
- Suspicious activity period: `{{start_date}}` to `{{end_date}}`
- Aggregate amount: `{{amount_usd}}`
- Primary typology: `{{typology}}`

## Narrative (5W + H)
> On `{{detection_date}}`, Atlas Bank detected `{{summary_of_activity}}` involving
> account `{{account_number}}` held by `{{subject_legal_name}}`. The activity
> aggregated `{{amount_usd}}` and is characterized as `{{typology}}` because
> `{{reason}}`. Funds `{{source_and_disposition}}`. The activity is inconsistent
> with the customer's expected profile (`{{expected_profile}}`).

## Required attachments
- Exception register entries supporting the filing.
- Transaction detail (dates, amounts, branches/counterparties).

## Approval
SAR filings require **BSA Officer** approval before submission to FinCEN.

# BSA/AML Regulatory Reference (Public Summary)

This non-confidential reference summarizes the principal U.S. anti-money-laundering
obligations relevant to Atlas Bank staff. It contains no customer information.

## Core obligations
- **Customer Identification Program (CIP):** verify the identity of each customer
  at account opening.
- **Customer Due Diligence (CDD):** understand the nature and purpose of customer
  relationships and develop a risk profile.
- **Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD):** apply heightened scrutiny to higher-risk
  customers, including those with complex ownership or exposure to high-risk
  jurisdictions.
- **Ongoing monitoring:** detect and report suspicious activity.

## Structuring
"Structuring" means breaking a large transaction into smaller amounts to evade the
$10,000 CTR reporting threshold. Structuring is itself a federal offense regardless
of whether the underlying funds are legitimate.

## Recordkeeping
Records supporting CTRs and SARs must be retained for **five years**.
